title: Urostachys picardae
intro: Urostachys picardae, also known as Picar's club moss, is a type of non-vascular, ancient plant species belonging to the Lycopodiophyta class, characterized by its small, scale-like leaves and distinctive club-like shape. Traditionally, Urostachys picardae has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its reported antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Urostachys picardae is commonly used in traditional herbal medicine and is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being incorporated into cough syrups and expectorant remedies.
taxonomy: Urostachys picardae is classified within the family Lomariopsidaceae. This family is a part of the order Polypodiales. Further classification places Urostachys picardae within the class Polypodiopsida and the division Pteridophyta. The class Polypodiopsida is a group of ferns, and Pteridophyta is the division that includes all ferns and fern allies. Urostachys picardae is also known to be a part of the genus Urostachys.
